parameters-en.Channel: Selects the amp channel--Normal or Lead.
parameters-en.Drive: Controls the amount of gain applied to the signal, which influences the level of distortion. This parameter affects both Normal and Lead channels.
parameters-en.Bass: Controls the low frequency EQ of the tonestack.
parameters-en.Mid: Controls the midrange frequency EQ of the tonestack.
parameters-en.Treble: Controls the high frequency EQ of the tonestack.
parameters-en.Presence: Boosts upper mid and high frequencies in the power amp, resulting in additional punch and bite.
parameters-en.Deep: When on, boosts the bass frequencies before the graphic EQ.
parameters-en.Level: Controls the overall level of the Amp block. Unlike Master Volume, it has no effect on the block's tone.
parameters-en.BassShift: When on, boosts the bass frequencies at the first gain stage.
parameters-en.TrebShift: When on, shifts the treble frequencies of the tone stack lower.
parameters-en.Bright: When on, applies a high frequency boost. This parameter interacts with the Drive parameter and affects both Normal and Lead channels.
parameters-en.LeadDrive: Controls the amount of gain applied to the signal, which influences the level of distortion in the Lead channel.
parameters-en.LeadBrite: When on, applies a high frequency boost. This parameter interacts with the LeadDrive parameter and affects only the Lead channel.
parameters-en.LeadMastr: Controls the Master Volume of the Lead channel of the amplifier.
parameters-en.Master: Controls the Master Volume of the amplifier. This parameter affects both Normal and Lead channels.
parameters-en.Power: Selects the topology of the power amp.
parameters-en.80 Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.240 Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.750 Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.2200 Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.6600 Hz: The amount of boost and cut is not equal for all bands. This is consistent with the real amp.
parameters-en.Sag: Controls the amount of sag, or how much the power supply compresses or "droops" in response to striking the strings hard. Higher values provide more touch dynamics, sustain, and organic feel that's inherent in vintage tube amps; lower values offer a "tighter" responsiveness for a more modern feel.
parameters-en.Ripple: Controls how much AC ripple interacts with your tone. Power amp circuits will sometimes let a little bit of rectified 120Hz hum (that the power supply filter caps can't quite fully remove) into the audio signal when the power supply is being pushed hard. Much like Hum, Ripple provides a bit of non-musical distortion to the power amp at distorted settings.
parameters-en.Z PrePost: Determines the location of speaker impedance characteristics in the power amp, primarily due to negative feedback. Higher values mean the effects of the interaction appear at the output of the power amp (Post) and lower values mean more of the effect is fed back to the input of the power amp (Pre).
parameters-en.Hype: Depending on the amp and/or amp settings, increasing Hype may subtly or dramatically adjust various behind-the-scenes parameters to make the amp sound and feel smoother, fuller, punchier, tighter, and/or more forgiving, but at the expense of accuracy.
